questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution directs the State to promote the welfare of the people?
questions.solution:
Article 38: Welfare of the People

Article 38 of the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP) directs the State to secure a social order for the promotion of the welfare of the people. It mandates the State to minimize inequalities in income, status, and opportunity among individuals and groups.

questions.question: What is the minimum age required to contest elections to Panchayats as per Article 243F?
questions.solution:
Minimum Age for Panchayats

According to Article 243F, the minimum age required to contest elections to a Panchayat is 21 years. This is notably lower than the 25-year requirement for the Lok Sabha or State Legislative Assemblies.

questions.question: Which of the following parts of the Indian Constitution deals with financial relations between the Centre and the States?
questions.solution:
Financial Relations (Part XII)

Part XII of the Indian Constitution (Articles 264 to 300) specifically deals with Finance, Property, Contracts, and Suits. It outlines the distribution of revenue between the Union and the States and the roles of the Finance Commission.

questions.question: Which of the following Parts of the Constitution was amended by the 97ᵗʰ Amendment to promote co-operative societies?
questions.solution:
97th Amendment and Part IXB

The 97th Constitutional Amendment Act (2011) gave constitutional status and protection to co-operative societies. It added Part IXB to the Constitution, ensuring democratic and professional management of such societies.

questions.question: Which of the following Amendments added the words 'Socialist' and 'Secular' to the Preamble?
questions.solution:
42nd Amendment: The Preamble

The 42nd Amendment Act (1976) is the only amendment ever made to the Preamble. It added three new words: 'Socialist', 'Secular', and 'Integrity', reflecting the social and political philosophy of the era.

questions.question: How are the one-third reserved seats for women distributed across different categories in local bodies?
questions.option_1: Only one-third of the general (unreserved) seats are reserved for women, excluding reserved-category seats.
questions.option_2: One-third reservation applies only to leadership positions such as Sarpanch or Chairperson.
questions.option_3: One-third of all seats, including those reserved for SC/ST/OBC categories, are reserved for women, allowing a seat to be reserved simultaneously for a woman and for an SC/ST/OBC candidate.
questions.option_4: One-third reservation applies only to rural Panchayats and not to urban municipal bodies.
questions.solution:
Horizontal Reservation for Women

In local bodies, the 1/3rd reservation for women is 'horizontal.' This means one-third of all seats—including those already reserved for SC, ST, and OBC categories—are reserved for women. A single seat can be reserved for both a specific category (like ST) and a woman (ST-Woman).

questions.question: According to the Citizenship Act, how many years must a person ordinarily reside in India to apply for citizenship by naturalisation under general conditions?
questions.solution:
Citizenship by Naturalisation

Under the Citizenship Act, 1955, a person seeking citizenship by naturalisation must have resided in India for a total of 12 years (11 years of residence over the previous 14 years, plus the 12 months immediately preceding the application).

questions.question: What makes basic rules in the Constitution effective?
questions.option_4: They are based on popular customs only.
questions.solution:
Legal Enforceability

Basic rules in the Constitution are effective because they are legally enforceable. This means that if these rules or rights (especially Fundamental Rights) are violated, citizens have the right to seek a remedy in a court of law.

questions.question: Which of the following Amendments clarified that the President must act in accordance with the advice of the Council of Ministers?
questions.solution:
President Bound by Advice

While the original Constitution was somewhat ambiguous, the 42nd Amendment (1976) made it explicitly clear that the President must act in accordance with the advice of the Council of Ministers. The 44th Amendment later added that the President could ask for reconsideration once.

questions.question: Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution provides for the composition of Municipalities?
questions.solution:
Article 243R: Municipal Composition

While Article 243Q deals with the constitution of Municipalities, Article 243R specifically provides for their composition, including the manner of election of members and the representation of chairpersons.

questions.question: The Immigration and Foreigners Bill 2025, repeals which of the following Acts?
questions.solution:
Immigration and Foreigners Bill 2025

The Immigration and Foreigners Bill 2025 was introduced to consolidate and modernize border management laws. It specifically repeals the Passport (Entry into India) Act, 1920, which was an antiquated colonial-era law governing the entry of persons into the country.

questions.question: Which body acts as the apex at the district level in Panchayati Raj?
questions.solution:
Zilla Parishad

The Zilla Parishad acts as the apex body of the three-tier Panchayati Raj system at the district level. It coordinates the activities of all the Panchayat Samitis (block level) in the district and manages development programs.

questions.question: Why is legal enforceability of rules important in a society?
questions.option_1: So people can break them without fear
questions.option_2: It provides assurance that others will follow the rules.
questions.option_3: It gives power to the police only.
questions.solution:
Assurance of Rule-Following

Legal enforceability is crucial because it provides assurance to every citizen that if they follow the rules, others will be held to the same standard. Without the threat of legal consequences, there would be no guarantee of social cooperation or safety.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles of State Policy was added by the Forty-fourth Amendment Act, 1978?
questions.option_1: Reduction of inequalities in income and status among individuals and groups
questions.option_2: Protection and improvement of the environment and wildlife
questions.option_3: Provision for free legal aid to the poor
questions.option_4: Participation of workers in industrial management
questions.solution:
44th Amendment and DPSP

The 44th Amendment Act (1978) added a clause to Article 38, directing the State to minimize inequalities in income and eliminate inequalities in status, facilities, and opportunities. (Options 2, 3, and 4 were added by the 42nd Amendment).

questions.question: Which of the following statements correctly distinguishes between the Cabinet and the Council of Ministers?
questions.option_1: The Cabinet is not part of the executive, but the Council is part of the executive
questions.option_2: The Cabinet is larger and includes all ministers, while the Council is a smaller group
questions.option_3: The Cabinet includes senior ministers, while the Council includes all categories of ministers
questions.option_4: The Council of Ministers does not include the Prime Minister
questions.solution:
Cabinet vs. Council of Ministers

The Council of Ministers is a larger body (60–80 ministers) including all ranks. The Cabinet is a smaller, more powerful inner circle consisting only of senior Cabinet-rank ministers who determine government policy.

questions.question: Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution defines the money bill?
questions.solution:
Article 110: Money Bill

Article 110 defines a Money Bill as one that deals exclusively with matters like taxation, government borrowing, or expenditure from the Consolidated Fund. The Speaker of the Lok Sabha has the final authority to certify a bill as a Money Bill.

questions.question: Which level of government generally legislates on matters such as police, public health and agriculture in India?
questions.solution:
State List Subjects

Under the Seventh Schedule, matters of local and regional importance—such as Police, Public Health, and Agriculture—fall under the State List. Therefore, State Legislatures have the primary power to make laws on these subjects.

questions.question: Which of the following Articles deals with the citizenship of people of Indian origin residing outside India?
questions.solution:
Article 8: Persons of Indian Origin

Article 8 deals with the citizenship of persons of Indian origin residing outside India. It allows them to become Indian citizens if they (or their parents/grandparents) were born in undivided India and they register with the relevant Indian diplomatic mission.

questions.question: Which of the following is a discretionary power of the President of India?
questions.option_1: Dissolving the Lok Sabha without advice
questions.option_2: Giving assent to a Money Bill
questions.option_3: Appointing the Chief Justice of India
questions.option_4: Appointing a Prime Minister when no party has a clear majority
questions.solution:
Discretionary Power of the President

In a parliamentary system, the President generally acts on advice. However, a key discretionary power is appointing a Prime Minister when no single party or alliance has a clear majority in the Lok Sabha. The President must then use their judgment to invite the leader most likely to prove a majority.

questions.question: Article 6 of the Constitution pertains to the citizenship of persons who migrated to India from which of the following territories?
questions.solution:
Article 6: Migrants from Pakistan

Article 6 pertains to the citizenship rights of persons who migrated to India from Pakistan before or after the commencement of the Constitution, provided they met certain residency or registration requirements.

questions.question: Article 323B of the Constitution of India (added by the 42nd Amendment) empowers the Parliament to establish tribunals for adjudication of matters related to certain subjects. Which of the following matters is specifically covered under Article 323B?
questions.option_2: Only industrial disputes between employers and workers
questions.option_3: Recruitment and service conditions of public servants (civil services)
questions.option_4: Matters such as taxation, foreign exchange, land reforms, etc., which can be assigned to tribunals by legislation
questions.solution:
Article 323B: Tribunals for Other Matters

While Article 323A is exclusively for administrative tribunals, Article 323B allows Parliament or State Legislatures to establish tribunals for other matters such as taxation, foreign exchange, import-export, land reforms, and urban property ceilings.

questions.question: Which of the following types of cases is NOT generally taken up by Lok Adalats in India?
questions.solution:
Cases Not in Lok Adalats

Lok Adalats are for settling disputes through compromise. They do not take up murder trials or other serious non-compoundable criminal offenses, as these are crimes against the State that cannot be settled by a simple agreement between parties.

questions.question: The 25ᵗʰ Amendment curtailed the right to property by amending which Article of Indian Constitution?
questions.solution:
25th Amendment and Article 31

The 25th Amendment Act (1971) significantly curtailed the fundamental right to property by amending Article 31. It substituted the word 'compensation' with 'amount' to limit the judiciary's power to review the adequacy of payments made for acquired property.

questions.question: After its 1971 electoral victory, Indira Gandhi’s government amended the Constitution to remove legal obstacles to abolishing which of the following privileges?
questions.option_1: Privy purses of former princely rulers
questions.solution:
Abolition of Privy Purses

In 1971, the government moved the 26th Amendment to abolish the Privy Purses (annual payments made to former princely rulers) and their titles. This was done to fulfill the 'Socialist' commitment of reducing unearned privileges and financial burdens on the state.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Gram Sabha is correct?
questions.option_1: It meets only once every five years.
questions.option_2: It consists only of elected members of the Gram Panchayat.
questions.option_3: It has authority to override the decisions of the Panchayat.
questions.option_4: It comprises all adult voters in the Panchayat area; its role is defined by state law.
questions.solution:
The Gram Sabha

The Gram Sabha is the foundation of the Panchayati Raj. It comprises all adult members registered as voters in the village. It is a permanent body that monitors the functioning of the elected Gram Panchayat, though its specific powers vary by state law.
